metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: Modern Horizons 3 is a complicated set offering extensive deckbuilding flexibility beyond the ten basic two-color archetypes. The exceptional mana-fixing through the Landscape cycle enables aggressive splashing and three-color configurations.
color_ranking: Tier 2: Azorius Energy, Selesnya Aggro, Dimir Draw, Golgari Modified
Tier 3: Izzet Energy, Simic Eldrazi, Orzhov Modified/Sacrifice

color_notes.Note: MH3 is archetype-driven rather than color-driven. Rank pairs and themes (Eldrazi, Energy, Modified) rather than individual colors. The Landscape cycle and Nightshade Dryad make 3-color decks comfortable.

key_gameplay_notes: The ten Sheltering Landscape cards provide untapped tri-land fixing superior to previous sets.
Splash generously — the fixing supports even greedy configurations.
Prioritize synergistic payoffs over individually powerful cards.
Writhing Chrysalis is format-warping; prioritize accordingly.
Board sweepers are rare; token strategies thrive.

mechanics.name: Eldrazi Spawn Tokens
mechanics.summary: Function as mana acceleration, chump blockers, and fuel for payoff cards like Writhing Chrysalis and Titans' Vanguard.
mechanics.performance: Core to Gruul Eldrazi (tier 1) and Simic Eldrazi (tier 3).

key_examples.name: Writhing Chrysalis
key_examples.note: The "mythic common"; single-handedly carries its archetype.

mechanics.name: Energy
mechanics.summary: Powers draw engines and removal through cards like Izzet Generatorium and various energy sinks.
mechanics.performance: Defines Azorius, Izzet, and Boros archetypes.

mechanics.name: Modified
mechanics.summary: Creatures with counters, equipment, or Auras trigger payoffs like Cursed Wombat and Expanding Ooze.
mechanics.performance: Anchors Golgari, Orzhov, and Selesnya archetypes.

mechanics.name: Sacrifice
mechanics.summary: Enables graveyard synergies and value through cards like Chthonian Nightmare and Accursed Marauder.

tier_1.name: Gruul Eldrazi
tier_1.strategy: Ramp into Eldrazi using Spawn tokens. Writhing Chrysalis is the centerpiece. Combine spawn generators with removal and bombs.

tier_1.name: Boros Aggro
tier_1.strategy: Fast beats with energy payoffs. Curve out cheap threats and finish with burn.

tier_2.name: Azorius Energy
tier_2.strategy: Control-oriented energy midrange. Trade efficiently while accumulating energy for late-game payoffs.

tier_2.name: Selesnya Aggro
tier_2.strategy: Modified-focused creatures. Counters and equipment scale threats into game-winners.

tier_2.name: Dimir Draw
tier_2.strategy: Card advantage through draw engines. Petrifying Meddler and Refurbished Familiar generate value.

tier_2.name: Golgari Modified
tier_2.strategy: Counter synergies with creature growth. Stack counters via Modified triggers and overwhelm the board.

tier_3.name: Izzet Energy
tier_3.strategy: Tempo-focused energy deck. Cheap spells fuel energy for bigger payoffs.

tier_3.name: Simic Eldrazi
tier_3.strategy: Alternative Eldrazi ramp strategy. Combines green ramp with blue card draw.

tier_3.name: Orzhov Modified / Sacrifice
tier_3.strategy: Sacrifice synergies with modified creatures. Chthonian Nightmare and Accursed Marauder enable repeated value.

tier_4.name: Rakdos Artifacts
tier_4.strategy: Artifact sacrifice synergies. Lacks density and consistency compared to other archetypes.

general_tips: The Landscape cycle enables comfortable mana bases even in three-color decks — splash freely.
Writhing Chrysalis is format-warping; prioritize over almost anything else in green.
Energy decks prefer focused synergies; modified decks scale well with splashing.
Eldrazi decks benefit from multiple spawn generators and removal.
Board sweepers are rare; token strategies thrive.
Find an open lane and make your cards work together cohesively — MH3 rewards coherent synergy-building.
